title: Soft Touch Baby Bear Comforter,Bear Security Blanket,Unisex Baby Gifts Comforter,Machine Washable Plush Toy comforter Boy Girl Gift Blankie (White bear)
features: SOFT AND CUDDLY: This Baby Bear Comforter made with 100% cozy plush fabric with beautiful embroidered detail, gentle for your baby's sensitive skin. Non-toxic. Free from harmful chemicals.This baby snuggle toy blanket gives your little one the comfort and security they need for optimal development.

EASY TO GRIP FOR BABIES& TODDLERS：The blanket measures 11.8 x 11.8 inches when flat, and approx 20 inches from the top of the little Bear's head to the bottom of the blanket. It is the ideal cuddle size for little babies& toddlers.

PROVIDE SECURITY SOOTHE: The baby comforter blanket is designed as intimate companionship for newborn babies when parents are not with them, is super comfortable and a transitional object to help babies feel safe and secure. Helping baby learn to self-soothe and adapt to the unfamiliar environment around them.
MACHINE WASHABLE: Made from the softest fabrics, the security blanket is machine washable on a gentle cycle. Do not iron, bleach or machine dry. Wash before use.

PERFECT GIFT IDEAS -This Bear Security Blanket will bring smile and comfort to your little cutie, and may become your baby’s favorite animal friend.Better yet, this eye-catching baby bedding plush blanket is a perfect Christmas socking suffers, baby shower gift or toy for your friend and relative's child!
description: The soothing towel is a growth partner that the baby relies on rubbing, grasping, pulling, pulling and biting to solve the desire for psychological comfort after leaving the mother. It is an indispensable playmate for the baby to promote sensory function, appease and stabilize the baby's crying mood, and is a comforting toy to accompany the baby to sleep safely. Soft toys can bring the baby a sense of security in psychology. From the perspective of ergonomics, our products are specially designed for newborn babies. They can play a good role in hearing, grasping and sense of the baby, so that the baby can cultivate perception from a young age.
